canonical_question: How do I design a payment processing system?
aliases: how to build a payment platform like Stripe

temporal_validity.last_breaking_change: PCI DSS v4.0.1 — all future-dated requirements became mandatory 2025-03-31

constraints: PCI DSS v4.0.1 compliance is mandatory for any system that stores, processes, or transmits cardholder data
Never store raw card numbers (PAN), CVV, or magnetic stripe data — use tokenization via a PCI-certified payment gateway
Every payment mutation must be idempotent — use client-generated idempotency keys to prevent double charges
Implement double-entry bookkeeping in your ledger — every transaction creates equal debit and credit entries
Never rely on synchronous payment confirmation — use webhooks with signature verification as the source of truth
All payment amounts must use integer minor units (cents/pence) — never floating-point arithmetic

skip_this_unit_if.condition: User needs to integrate a single payment gateway into an existing app without designing the full system
skip_this_unit_if.use_instead: Stripe/Adyen/Braintree official integration docs for your platform

skip_this_unit_if.condition: User needs PCI DSS compliance guidance without system architecture
skip_this_unit_if.use_instead: PCI Security Standards Council document library (pcisecuritystandards.org)

skip_this_unit_if.condition: User needs to design a cryptocurrency or blockchain-based payment system

inputs_needed.question: What is the expected transaction volume?
Which payment methods need to be supported?
options: Full multi-method (cards, wallets, ACH, SEPA, crypto)

inputs_needed.question: What regions does the system need to serve?
